title: CCC
text: Home * Computer Chess Forums * Computer Chess Club (CCC) The Computer Chess Club (CCC, TC, TalkChess) is a moderated computer chess forum , established since 1997. Contents 1 Discrimination 2 CCC Links 3 CCC History 3.1 The Founders 3.2 Second Founders 3.3 Moderators 4 Forum Posts 4.1 1997 4.2 1998 4.3 1999 4.4 2000 ... 4.5 2010 ... 4.6 2020 ... 4.7 2024 ... 5 External Links 6 References Discrimination [ edit ] From May 19, 2020 at latest to February 29, 2024, the forum was blocked for several IP-ranges outside the US [1] for DDoS protection. See Appeal by Pawel Koziol . CCC Links [ edit ] Computer Chess Club (CCC) CCC - General Topics CCC - Programming and Technical Discussions CCC - Tournaments and Matches Computer Chess Club Archives hosted by Sean T Mintz [2] [3] CCC September/October 1997 CCC History [ edit ] CCC was established in October 1997 by the group of "founders", basically to get rid from flaming in rgcc [4] . The forum was used its own code developed by Steven Schwartz . Testing of the forum software already occurred in September. CCC has been hosted by IcdChess, ChessUSA [5] and firstly located at http://www.icdchess.com . Due to the limit of the server, old contents were frequently removed, saved as backups, and stored in other servers, mostly in the FTP server of Robert Hyatt . In 2006 the forum was changed to use phpBB code (a freeware forum), upgraded the server, and moved to a new domain at http://www.talkchess.com . However, all old contents could not be converted and the forum was continued with a totally new, empty database. Sean T Mintz had published old contents as an archive one [6] . Since February 29, 2024 the forum is in its third incarnation, lead by a second "founders group" and supported by the community members [7] . The Founders [ edit ] [8] Moritz Berger Thorsten Czub Dirk Frickenschmidt Bob Hyatt Enrique Irazoqui Andreas Mader Bruce Moreland Peter Schreiner Ed Schröder Chris Whittington Second Founders [ edit ] [9] Ed Schröder Chris Whittington Harm Geert Muller Moderators [ edit ] Three moderators were periodically elected, first by the founders [10] , since May 1998 by member voting from a pool of nominees [11] .
